trajectory.description: Observation: Baseline ResNet50 achieved test accuracy 70.54% with 5 epochs on noisy labels. Analysis: Label noise (~40%) limits performance; standard cross-entropy overfits to noise. Decision: Implement cleanlab-based cleaning pipeline: OOF cross-validated probabilities to detect noisy samples, then train final model only on clean subset with label smoothing.

trajectory.description: Observation: Iter 2 cleanlab pipeline bug: 'Identified 415797251 noisy labels out of 50000 (831594.5%)' — clearly incorrect, likely due to wrong usage of find_label_issues or bad pred_probs. No eval submitted, wasted iteration. Analysis: The OOF cross-validation code may have mishandled probabilities or label arrays. Need to rewrite train.py with correct cleanlab usage, add proper logging, and train final model on cleaned data. Decision: Rewrite train.py from scratch with robust 2-fold cross-validation, correct find_label_issues call, clean data filtering, then final training with label smoothing and longer epochs. Also update predict.py to support multiple backbones via checkpoint metadata. Submit eval after successful training to measure improvement over baseline 0.7054.

trajectory.description: Observation: Test accuracy 0.711 (best so far) with ResNet50 + cleanlab cleaning. Val accuracy plateaued at 78.7% but test gap indicates overfitting or poor cleaning. Analysis: 1) OOF used only 5 epochs per fold, insufficient for good probability estimates. 2) ResNet50 may not be the best backbone for noisy-label robustness. 3) No mixup augmentation, leading to overfitting. Decision: Upgrade to ConvNeXt Tiny backbone, increase OOF epochs to 10, add mixup (alpha=0.2) during final training, use SWA for better generalization, filter noisy labels more conservatively (keep samples with label_quality≥0.5 and cleanlab issues=False), train for 50 epochs. Submit eval after training to measure improvement.

trajectory.description: Observation: Current best test accuracy 0.7238 with ConvNeXt Tiny + cleanlab cleaning + mixup + SWA. Val accuracy 96% but huge test gap suggests severe overfitting to cleaned subset or poor cleaning. The OOF probability estimation may be insufficient (only 10 epochs per fold). Analysis: (1) Cleaning might keep too few easy samples causing overfitting. (2) Noise‑robust losses like Generalized Cross Entropy (GCE) can directly train on full noisy data with mixup, avoiding the OOF step entirely and potentially improving generalization. This also saves time. Decision: Replace cleaning pipeline with a simple GCE‑based training loop on all data, with mixup, SWA, and ConvNeXt Tiny. Train for 60 epochs and submit eval to measure improvement.

We can leverage the existing best checkpoint (likely swa_epoch_50.pt) and submit a new evaluation with TTA, then if improved, plan further training (e.g., small-loss finetuning or ViT backbone).
